chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Etiquettes of the Fasting Person, What Invalidates the Fast, and What Does Not Invalidate it

text: 1855 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «إِذَا صُمْتَ فَلْيَصُمْ سَمْعُكَ وَ بَصَرُكَ وَ شَعْرُكَ وَ جِلْدُكَ وَ عَدَّدَ أَشْيَاءَ غَيْرَ هَذَا» وَ قَالَ «لاَ يَكُونُ يَوْمُ صَوْمِكَ كَيَوْمِ فِطْرِكَ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1855 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam (as) that Imam (as) said:
"When you fast, let your hearing, your sight, your hair, and your skin also fast," and Imam mentioned other things besides these.
Imam (as) also said: "The day of your fast should not be like the day when you are not fasting."
